intro: Atelophragma ibapense is a species of flowering plant in the family Aristolochiaceae, commonly known as the madhuca vine or Aristolochia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in the treatment of various conditions, including wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Atelophragma ibapense include alkaloids, particularly aristolochic acid, as well as flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its therapeutic properties.
